beats.narration: The radiograph you shot this morning may face an audit twenty years from now. Not just a picture. A quality record.

payload.title: THE FILM ARCHIVE
payload.sub: A radiograph is a quality record built to last decades.

beats.narration: That takes three jobs: process the film chemically clean, store it cool and dry, and file it so anyone can find it.

payload.heading: Three jobs keep a radiograph alive

beats.narration: Leftover fixer, residual thiosulfate, slowly converts the silver image to yellow-brown silver sulfide. A spot test catches it: darker stain, more hypo left.
Store films cool and dry: sixty to seventy-five degrees Fahrenheit, thirty to fifty percent humidity. File on edge, never flat stacks, and index everything.
Process it clean, store it cool, file it findable — a record you can't produce in minutes is a record you don't have.

payload.sub: Produce any film in minutes — not days.
